title_en: Taharah in Adh-Dhakhirah: Al-Qarafi's Analytical Treatment
title_id: Taharah dalam Adh-Dhakhirah: Pendekatan Analitis Al-Qarafi
content_en:
The taharah chapter of Adh-Dhakhirah demonstrates al-Qarafi's characteristic analytical approach: engaging with the evidential basis of each ruling, evaluating conflicting opinions within and across schools, and offering his own assessments where the evidence allows.

Al-Qarafi's treatment of water classification begins with a conceptual discussion of what makes water suitable for ritual purification — a discussion more philosophical than most fiqh texts would include. He explains that the criterion is not a fixed physical property of the water but a combination of physical properties and legal designations that reflect the Lawgiver's intent to make purification accessible while maintaining standards that protect the dignity of worship.

On the question of the amount of standing water below which contact with najasah renders it impure, al-Qarafi engages with both the Maliki practical-assessment approach and the Shafi'i two-qullah threshold, evaluating the evidential basis for each. He notes that the Maliki approach — treating the question as a matter of practical assessment based on abundance versus scarcity — may actually produce more accurate results in specific cases than the fixed threshold, since the relevant concern is whether the water retains its purifying character despite the contact with impurity.

The wudu chapter in Adh-Dhakhirah covers the Maliki obligations with careful attention to their evidential basis. On the requirement of muwalah (continuity), al-Qarafi engages with the evidence for and against treating it as obligatory. He presents the Maliki position — that continuity is required to the point that the earlier washed parts should not have dried before the later ones are washed, in a moderate climate — as well-grounded in the transmitted practice of the Prophet and Medina, while acknowledging the evidential case for those who treat it as recommended.

On ghusl's obligatory rubbing, Adh-Dhakhirah provides al-Qarafi's characteristic engagement with the evidence. The Maliki requirement of dalk (rubbing) is grounded in the language of the Quran (verse 4:43 uses the word 'wash' which implies physical contact) and the consistent practice of the Prophet and Medina as Malik observed it. Al-Qarafi defends this interpretation against the majority who regard rubbing as recommended rather than obligatory.

The chapter on hayd and nifas in Adh-Dhakhirah is particularly detailed, reflecting al-Qarafi's attention to the questions that arise in practice. He addresses the minimum and maximum durations, the characteristics that distinguish menstrual blood from other types, and the acts forbidden to a woman during hayd — covering both the standard positions and the edge cases that require careful reasoning.
content_id:
Bab taharah dalam Adh-Dhakhirah memperlihatkan pendekatan analitis khas al-Qarafi: terlibat dengan dasar dalil setiap ketentuan hukum, mengevaluasi pendapat-pendapat yang bertentangan di dalam dan lintas mazhab, serta menawarkan penilaiannya sendiri di mana dalil memungkinkan.

Pembahasan al-Qarafi tentang klasifikasi air dimulai dengan diskusi konseptual tentang apa yang membuat air cocok untuk bersuci secara ritual, sebuah diskusi yang lebih filosofis daripada yang biasanya disertakan oleh kebanyakan teks fikih. Ia menjelaskan bahwa kriterianya bukan sifat fisik air yang baku melainkan kombinasi antara sifat fisik dan penetapan hukum yang mencerminkan niat Pembuat Syariat untuk membuat bersuci mudah diakses sambil mempertahankan standar yang melindungi kemuliaan ibadah.

Mengenai pertanyaan tentang jumlah air tergenang yang di bawahnya kontak dengan najis menjadikannya najis, al-Qarafi terlibat dengan pendekatan penilaian praktis Maliki dan ambang batas dua qullah Syafi'i, mengevaluasi dasar dalil untuk masing-masing. Ia mencatat bahwa pendekatan Maliki, yakni memperlakukan pertanyaan sebagai masalah penilaian praktis berdasarkan kelimpahan versus kelangkaan, mungkin menghasilkan hasil yang lebih akurat dalam kasus-kasus tertentu dibandingkan ambang batas yang baku, karena keprihatinan yang relevan adalah apakah air mempertahankan karakter penyuciannya meskipun telah berkontak dengan najis.

Bab wudu dalam Adh-Dhakhirah mencakup kewajiban-kewajiban Maliki dengan perhatian cermat terhadap dasar dalilnya. Mengenai persyaratan muwalah (kesinambungan), al-Qarafi terlibat dengan dalil yang mendukung dan menentang perlakuannya sebagai kewajiban. Ia menyajikan posisi Maliki bahwa kesinambungan diperlukan sampai pada titik bahwa anggota yang lebih awal dicuci tidak boleh mengering sebelum anggota-anggota berikutnya dicuci dalam iklim sedang, sebagai sesuatu yang berdasar kuat pada praktik Nabi dan Madinah yang ditransmisikan, sambil mengakui argumen dalil bagi mereka yang memperlakukannya sebagai anjuran.

Mengenai menggosok yang wajib dalam ghusl, Adh-Dhakhirah memberikan keterlibatan khas al-Qarafi dengan dalil. Persyaratan Maliki berupa dalk (menggosok) didasarkan pada bahasa Al-Quran, ayat 4:43 menggunakan kata mandi yang mengimplikasikan kontak fisik, dan praktik konsisten Nabi serta Madinah sebagaimana yang diamati oleh Imam Malik. Al-Qarafi mempertahankan penafsiran ini terhadap mayoritas ulama yang menganggap menggosok sebagai anjuran alih-alih kewajiban.

Bab tentang haid dan nifas dalam Adh-Dhakhirah sangat terperinci, mencerminkan perhatian al-Qarafi terhadap persoalan-persoalan yang muncul dalam praktik. Ia membahas durasi minimum dan maksimum, karakteristik yang membedakan darah haid dari jenis-jenis darah lainnya, dan perbuatan-perbuatan yang dilarang bagi wanita selama haid, mencakup baik posisi-posisi standar maupun kasus-kasus tepi yang memerlukan penalaran yang cermat.
content_ar: The الطهارة الباب of Adh-Dhakhirah demonstrates al-Qarafi's characteristic analytical approach: engaging with the evidential basis of each ruling, evaluating conflicting opinions within and across schools, and offering his own assessments where the evidence allows. Al-Qarafi's treatment of water classification begins with a conceptual discussion of what makes water suitable for ritual purification — a discussion more philosophical than most الفقه texts would include. He explains that the criterion is not a fixed physical property of the water but a combination of physical properties and legal designations that reflect the Lawgiver's intent to make purification accessible بينما maintaining standards that protect the الكرامة of worship. On the question of the amount of standing water below which contact with النجاسة renders it impure, al-Qarafi engages with both the المالكي practical-assessment approach and the الشافعي two-qullah threshold, evaluating the evidential basis for each. He notes that the المالكي approach — treating the question as a matter of practical assessment based on abundance versus scarcity — may actually produce more accurate results in specific cases than the fixed threshold, بما أن the relevant concern is whether the water retains its purifying الأخلاق رغم the contact with impurity. The الوضوء الباب in Adh-Dhakhirah covers the المالكي obligations with careful attention to their evidential basis. On the requirement of muwalah (continuity), al-Qarafi engages with the evidence for and against treating it as obligatory. He presents the المالكي position — that continuity is required to the point that the earlier washed parts should not have dried قبل the later ones are washed, in a moderate climate — as well-grounded in the transmitted practice of النبي ﷺ and المدينة المنورة, بينما acknowledging the evidential case for those who treat it as recommended. On الغسل's obligatory rubbing, Adh-Dhakhirah provides al-Qarafi's characteristic engagement with the evidence. The المالكي requirement of dalk (rubbing) is grounded in the language of the القرآن الكريم (الآية 4:43 uses the word 'wash' which implies physical contact) and the consistent practice of النبي ﷺ and المدينة المنورة as مالك observed it. Al-Qarafi defends this interpretation against the majority who regard rubbing as recommended rather than obligatory. The الباب on hayd and nifas in Adh-Dhakhirah is particularly detailed, reflecting al-Qarafi's attention to the questions that arise in practice. He addresses the minimum and maximum durations, the characteristics that distinguish menstrual blood from other types, and the acts forbidden to a woman خلال hayd — covering both the standard positions and the edge cases that require careful reasoning.
